content:
# Matthew Chapter 24 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_024_page_001.jpg, MAT_024_page_002.jpg

1. And having gone out, the Yeshua was going from the temple, and the disciples of him came to show him the buildings of the temple.

2. And answering, he said to them, "Do you not see all these things? Truly I say to you, there will not be left here stone upon stone which will not be thrown down."

3. And of him sitting upon the Mount of the Olives, the disciples came to him privately, saying, "Tell us, when will these things be, and what [is] the sign of the coming of you and of [the] completion of the age?"

4. And answering, the Yeshua said to them, "Watch out that no one deceives you.

5. For many will come in the name of me, saying, 'I am the Christos,' and they will deceive many.

6. And you will hear of wars and rumors of wars. See [that] you are not troubled; for it is necessary [for these] to happen, but the end is not yet.

7. For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom, and there will be famines and earthquakes in various places.

8. But all these [are] a beginning of birth-pains.

9. Then they will deliver you to tribulation and will kill you, and you will be hated by all the nations on account of the name of me.

10. And then many will be scandalized and will betray one another and will hate one another.

11. And many false-prophets will arise and will deceive many.

12. And because of the increase of the lawlessness, the love of the many will grow cold.

13. But the [one] having endured to [the] end, this [one] will be saved.

14. And this gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed in the whole inhabited-world for a testimony to all the nations, and then will come the end.

15. When therefore you see the abomination of the desolation, the [one] having been spoken through Daniel the prophet, standing in [the] holy place — the [one] reading let him understand —

16. then the [ones] in the Ioudaia, let them flee to the mountains.

17. The [one] upon the housetop, let him not come down to take the things from the house of him.

18. And the [one] in the field, let him not turn back to take the garment of him.

19. And woe to the [ones] having [a child] in [the] womb and the [ones] nursing in those days.

20. But pray that the flight of you may not be in winter nor on a Sabbath.

21. For then there will be great tribulation, such as has not happened from [the] beginning of [the] world until now, nor ever will happen.

22. And unless those days were shortened, no flesh would be saved; but on account of the chosen, those days will be shortened.

23. Then if anyone says to you, 'Behold, here [is] the Christos,' or 'Here,' do not believe.

24. For false-Christoi and false-prophets will arise and will give great signs and wonders, so as to deceive, if possible, even the chosen.

25. Behold, I have told you beforehand.

26. If therefore they say to you, 'Behold, he is in the wilderness,' do not go out; 'Behold, in the inner rooms,' do not believe.

27. For just as the lightning comes from [the] east and appears to [the] west, so will be the coming of the Son of the Man.

28. Wherever the corpse is, there the eagles will be gathered.

29. And immediately after the tribulation of those days, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give the light of it, and the stars will fall from the heaven, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.

30. And then will appear the sign of the Son of the Man in heaven, and then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of the Man coming upon the clouds of the heaven with power and much glory.

31. And he will send the angels of him with a great trumpet, and they will gather the chosen of him from the four winds, from [one] end of [the] heavens to [the other] end of them.

32. And from the fig tree learn the parable: when the branch of it already becomes tender and the leaves sprout, you know that the summer [is] near.

33. So also you, when you see all these things, know that it is near, at [the] doors.

34. Truly I say to you that this generation will not pass away until all these things happen.

35. The heaven and the earth will pass away, but the words of me will not pass away.

36. But concerning that day and hour, no one knows, neither the angels of the heavens nor the Son, except the Father alone.

37. For just as the days of Noah, so will be the coming of the Son of the Man.

38. For as they were in the days before the flood, eating and drinking, marrying and giving-in-marriage, until [the] day Noah entered into the ark,

39. and they did not know until the flood came and took all, so will be the coming of the Son of the Man.

40. Then two will be in the field; one is taken and one is left.

41. Two [women] grinding at the mill; one is taken and one is left.

42. Watch therefore, for you do not know on what day the Lord of you comes.

43. But know this: that if the householder had known in what watch the thief comes, he would have watched and would not have permitted the house of him to be broken into.

44. Because of this, also you be ready, for in [the] hour [that] you do not think, the Son of the Man comes.

45. Who then is the faithful and wise slave, whom the lord set over the household of him, to give to them the food in [the right] season?

46. Blessed [is] the slave that whom the lord of him, having come, will find so doing.

47. Truly I say to you that over all the possessions of him he will set him.

48. But if the evil slave that says in the heart of him, 'The lord of me delays,'

49. and begins to beat the fellow-slaves of him, and eats and drinks with the [ones] being drunk,

50. the lord of the slave that will come on a day on which he does not expect and in an hour which he does not know,

51. and will cut him in two and will place the portion of him with the hypocrites; there will be the weeping and the gnashing of the teeth.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **παρουσία (vv.3, 27, 37, 39):** "Coming/presence" — the word παρουσία means both "coming" and "presence." In Hellenistic Greek, it was used for the official visit of a king or emperor. Jesus' "parousia" is the royal arrival of the Son of Man.

2. **v.15 — "Abomination of desolation":** τὸ βδέλυγμα τῆς ἐρημώσεως — from Daniel 9:27, 11:31, 12:11. Originally referring to the desecration of the temple by Antiochus IV Epiphanes (167 BCE). Jesus applies it to a future desecration.

3. **v.28 — "eagles":** ἀετοί can mean eagles or vultures. The saying is proverbial: where the carcass is, the scavengers gather. In context, it may allude to Roman military standards (which bore eagle insignia) gathering around the doomed city.

4. **v.36 — "nor the Son":** Sinaiticus includes "nor the Son" (οὐδὲ ὁ υἱός). Some later manuscripts omit this phrase, likely for theological reasons (reluctance to attribute ignorance to Jesus). The Sinaiticus reading preserves Jesus' candid admission of limited knowledge regarding the timing.

5. **v.34 — "this generation":** γενεὰ αὕτη — one of the most debated phrases in the NT. Does it mean the generation alive at the time, or a "type" of people? The Sinaiticus text gives no variant.

— EMET, AI Translator
